findings.title: XSS in dashboard fetchPRs error handler missing esc()
findings.detail: In dashboard/index.html fetchPRs(), the catch block interpolates e.message directly into innerHTML without calling esc() (line 462). Four other similar error handlers in the same file correctly use esc(e.message). If the error message contains HTML characters — which is plausible from network errors or GitHub API status text — it would be executed as HTML in the operator's browser.
findings.fix_sketch: Change `${e.message}` to `${esc(e.message)}` in the catch block at line 462.

findings.title: XSS in dashboard fetchCommits error handler missing esc()
findings.detail: In dashboard/index.html fetchCommits(), the catch block interpolates e.message directly into innerHTML without calling esc() (line 483). This is the same pattern as the fetchPRs vulnerability. Both error handlers were missed when the other four were fixed.
findings.fix_sketch: Change `${e.message}` to `${esc(e.message)}` in the catch block at line 483.

findings.title: Private field access via as-any cast in build-cards.ts
findings.detail: src/learning/reviewer-prefs/build-cards.ts line 183 uses `(store as any).db` to access the private `db` field of `TaskStore`. This bypasses TypeScript access controls and is fragile — a refactor of TaskStore internals will silently break the reviewer card pipeline at runtime with no type-system warning. TaskStore has `getPrDecisionsByRepo()` but lacks a method that queries across all repos with custom ORDER BY/LIMIT, which is what build-cards needs.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a public `listAllPrDecisions(opts: { windowMs: number; limit?: number; repo?: string }): PrDecision[]` method to TaskStore. Replace the as-any cast in build-cards.ts with a call to the new method.

findings.title: Trailing whitespace in scorer.ts defaultWarn function
findings.detail: src/agents/proposer/scorer.ts line 192 has 3 trailing spaces on an otherwise blank line inside the defaultWarn function body. This causes no functional issue but pollutes diffs and may trip linters.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the trailing spaces from line 192.

findings.title: User task content exposed in process list via CLI argv
findings.detail: In `defaultHaikuCall`, the full auto-router prompt (which includes the user-supplied task title and brief body) is passed as a positional CLI argument: `execFile('claude', ['--print', prompt, '--model', model], ...)`. On Linux, any process with read access to `/proc` or running `ps aux` can read the full prompt, including user task descriptions. The comment at L290-299 acknowledges this and calls it 'not secret material', but the user brief can contain PII or internal project details. The same pattern is used in `task-done-notify.ts` (L42).
findings.fix_sketch: Pass the prompt via stdin instead of argv. Open a write-pipe to the child process's stdin and write the prompt there, then close stdin. Use the `--dangerously-skip-permissions` or `--print -` variant if the CLI supports reading from stdin. Alternatively, write the prompt to a temp file (0600 perms) and pass a `--file` flag if the CLI supports it.

findings.title: Hung subprocess holds worker slot forever — no timeout on awaitHandle
findings.detail: In `SprintManager.awaitHandle()`, the code does `const result = await entry.handle.done` with no timeout. If the subprocess becomes unresponsive to both SIGTERM and SIGKILL (e.g. kernel-level hang, zombie process), this Promise never settles. The worker slot is held indefinitely, the sprint never completes or fails, and the task appears `in_flight` in the store forever. The `cancel()` function in `spawn-runner.ts` sends SIGTERM then waits `graceMs`=5s then sends SIGKILL, but `awaitHandle` is called after `cancel()` has already returned — so it re-awaits the same never-settling promise.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap `entry.handle.done` in a `Promise.race` with a hard timeout (e.g. 30 min for typical sprint length). On timeout, log a critical event, force-release the worker slot via `this.registry.release(entry.workerId)`, mark the task failed in the store, and emit `sprint.failed`. Use `AbortController` or `setTimeout`-based rejection.

findings.title: Docker verifier runs as root — container privilege escalation risk
findings.detail: `buildDockerArgs()` hardcodes `'--user', 'root'` for all Docker sandbox runs. If the Docker image or any tool it runs has a vulnerability, the attacker has root inside the container. With a volume mount of the worktree at `/work`, a root process can write files with root ownership that persist on the host filesystem after the container exits. This is especially dangerous for the install phase which runs with `--network bridge` and downloads arbitrary npm packages.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace `'--user', 'root'` with a non-root UID, e.g. `'--user', '1000:1000'` or a named user created in the Dockerfile. Ensure the `/work` volume mount is readable/writable by that UID. For pnpm store (`/root/.pnpm-store`), change the store path to `/home/node/.pnpm-store` or another non-root-owned directory in the image. Also consider adding `--cap-drop ALL --security-opt no-new-privileges`.

findings.title: markCancelled calls markFailed — deliberate cancels appear as failures externally
findings.detail: In `UnifiedQueueAdapter.markCancelled()`, the unified store correctly writes state='blocked' with `cancelled:true`, but the source-side notification calls `this.sourceFor(task).markFailed(task, reason)`. This means GitHub issues get a `failed` label and Discord threads get a red `Failed` embed when the operator deliberately cancels a task via `/cancel` or `/stop`. The comment acknowledges this as a known tradeoff but it causes operator confusion and contaminates bandit/canary disagreement metrics.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a `markCancelled(task: QueuedTask, reason: string): Promise<void>` method to the `TaskSource` interface (with a default no-op for backward compat). Implement it in `GitHubSource` to apply a `cancelled` label instead of `failed`. Implement it in `DiscordSource` to post a `🛑 Cancelled` embed. Then call it here instead of `markFailed`.

findings.title: unifiedToPipelineTask passes empty labels — routing hints not propagated from Discord tasks
findings.detail: In `pipeline-bridge.ts:unifiedToPipelineTask()`, the `labels` field is always set to `[]`. Discord-sourced tasks store routing hints in `task.routingHints` (autonomy, category, severity, verify, priority) but these are never mapped to the legacy pipeline `labels` array. The `classifyTask()` scorer reads `task.labels` to detect `category:*`, `severity:*`, `complexity:high/low`, and `chore`/`docs` downgrade signals. Discord tasks therefore skip all label-driven routing overrides.
findings.fix_sketch: Map `task.routingHints` to synthetic labels before passing to the pipeline. E.g. if `routingHints.category === 'security'` push `'category:security'`; if `routingHints.severity === 'critical'` push `'severity:critical'`; if `routingHints.priority === 'high'` push `'priority:high'`.

findings.title: sprint-bridge passes empty string prUrl for tasks that produced no PR
findings.detail: In `wireSprintCompletion`, when a sprint completes but `lastPrUrl` is null/undefined (no changes needed), the call is `adapter.markCompleted(task, lastPrUrl ?? '', lastTotalTokens)`. The empty-string `prUrl` causes `DiscordOutAdapter.postCompleted` to emit 'Pull request opened.' followed by an empty URL, confusing operators.
findings.fix_sketch: Define a sentinel constant `NO_PR_SENTINEL = 'no-pr'` in `contracts/discord-out.ts`. Pass it here and check for it in `DiscordOutAdapter.postCompleted` to render 'No changes needed — task closed without a PR.'

findings.title: cancel() in spawn-runner always waits full graceMs even when process already exited
findings.detail: The `cancel()` function does `child.kill('SIGTERM')` then `await delay(graceMs)` (default 5000ms) unconditionally. If the child exits immediately on SIGTERM (the common case for well-behaved processes), the caller still waits the full 5 seconds before control returns. This serializes all sprint cancellations and `/stop` commands, adding up to 5s latency per task.
findings.fix_sketch: Race the delay against the process exit: `await Promise.race([delay(graceMs), new Promise(r => child.once('close', r))])`. This lets fast-exiting processes complete immediately while still enforcing the grace timeout for slow ones.

findings.title: AnthropicCompleter silently fails if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set
findings.detail: In `candidate-gen.ts`, `AnthropicCompleter` reads `process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` directly in its constructor. If the key is missing, it throws with a helpful message, but this throw happens inside `generateCandidates()` which wraps it in a try/catch that returns an empty array with a `warn()` call. The proposer silently produces zero candidates with no operator-visible error.
findings.fix_sketch: Emit a CRITICAL-level Discord broadcast when `AnthropicCompleter` construction fails, so the operator knows the proposer is non-functional. Alternatively, validate `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` at startup in `proposer-run.ts` and exit with code 1 if missing, so PM2 marks the process as errored.

findings.title: Supabase TLS verification disabled when SUPABASE_CA_CERT_PATH is not set
findings.detail: In `pg-client.ts`, the Postgres connection to Supabase uses `{ rejectUnauthorized: false }` when `SUPABASE_CA_CERT_PATH` is not configured. The comment says this is 'acceptable only for Supabase's managed TLS'. However, disabling certificate verification makes the connection vulnerable to MITM attacks on the Postgres traffic, which carries schema data, code symbol embeddings, and potentially task metadata.
findings.fix_sketch: Bundle the Supabase root CA certificate in the repo or in a secret, set `SUPABASE_CA_CERT_PATH` in the PM2 ecosystem config, and enforce `rejectUnauthorized: true` in production. Add a startup check that warns loudly if the CA path is unset when connecting to a `supabase.co` host.

findings.title: Proposal button auth falls back to union of all allowedUserIds when IFLEET_PROPOSALS_APPROVER_IDS is unset
findings.detail: In `interaction-create.ts`, when `IFLEET_PROPOSALS_CHANNEL_ID` matches but `IFLEET_PROPOSALS_APPROVER_IDS` is not set, the auth check falls back to the union of all `allowedUserIds` across all channel routes. This means every Discord user who can submit tasks in any channel can also approve proposals — a broader grant than intended.
findings.fix_sketch: When `IFLEET_PROPOSALS_APPROVER_IDS` is unset, fail closed (deny all non-explicit approvals with a Discord reply explaining the missing config) or log a WARN at startup and document that proposal approval is open to all allowed users. Do not silently expand the permission scope.

findings.title: postWebhook in task-done-notify has no request timeout — can hang indefinitely
findings.detail: In `task-done-notify.ts`, `postWebhook()` uses `node:https` directly with no `timeout` set on the `request` object. If the Discord webhook server is slow or unreachable, the HTTP request hangs with no deadline, blocking the completion callback for potentially 2+ minutes.
findings.fix_sketch: Add `req.setTimeout(10_000, () => req.destroy(new Error('webhook timeout')))` before `req.write(body)`.

findings.title: sendNow in discord-broadcast has no HTTP request timeout
findings.detail: The `sendNow` and `sendNowAsync` functions in `discord-broadcast.ts` use `node:https` / `node:http` directly with no request timeout. A Discord API outage that causes connection-level hangs would cause all broadcast calls to queue behind the hung connection until the grace timer expires.
findings.fix_sketch: Add `req.setTimeout(8_000, () => req.destroy())` in both `sendNow` and `sendNowAsync`, matching the Langfuse/Postgres timeouts in the codebase.

findings.title: Fingerprint computation hardcodes baseRef 'main' instead of using bootstrap.input.baseBranch
findings.detail: In `wrapFactoryWithVerifierContext()` teardown, the structural fingerprint is computed with `baseRef: 'main'` hardcoded. A comment says 'safe: production worktrees are always created from main'. However, `bootstrap.input.baseBranch` is available in context and could differ for ad-hoc worktrees or future feature-branch sprint support. If a sprint runs against a non-main base branch, the fingerprint diff will be over the wrong range.
findings.fix_sketch: Use `bootstrap.input.baseBranch ?? 'main'` as the `baseRef`. If not set, fall back to `'main'`.

findings.title: Hardcoded dated model string 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' in candidate-gen.ts
findings.detail: The `HAIKU_MODEL` constant in `candidate-gen.ts` is hardcoded to `'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'`. Model strings are managed via `TIERS` in `classifier/index.ts` which reads from `config/routing.json`. The proposer bypasses this routing layer and will silently use an outdated model string when the canonical tier mapping is updated.
findings.fix_sketch: Import `FALLBACK_TIERS` or `TIERS` from `classifier/index.ts` and use `TIERS.haiku` instead of the hardcoded string.

findings.title: Unused octokit field retained in HandleForcePrDeps for compile compatibility
findings.detail: In `pr-decisions.ts`, the `HandleForcePrDeps` interface retains an `octokit` field documented as unused: 'retained as an optional, UNUSED field purely so the existing daemon wiring keeps compiling without an out-of-lane edit'. This dead field adds noise and will confuse future maintainers.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the `octokit` field from `HandleForcePrDeps` and update the daemon wiring to stop passing it.

findings.title: parseClaudeEvent iterates evt.message.content without Array.isArray check
findings.detail: In `claude.ts`, the `type === 'assistant'` branch iterates `evt.message.content` with `for...of` without checking `Array.isArray(content)` first. If the API returns `content` as a non-array, this throws a `TypeError: evt.message.content is not iterable` that propagates as a worker crash.
findings.fix_sketch: Change `if (type === 'assistant' && evt.message?.content)` to `if (type === 'assistant' && Array.isArray(evt.message?.content))` to guard the for...of loop.

findings.title: Module-level side effect throws on import when a mode prompt exceeds 500 chars
findings.detail: In `classifier/modes.ts` (L108-113), a `for...of Object.entries(PROMPTS)` loop at module load time throws `Error` if any prompt exceeds 500 characters. A module-level throw crashes the entire process on import, including during tests that import other classifier modules.
findings.fix_sketch: Move the check to a `validateModePrompts()` function called once at daemon startup. In test environments, the check can be skipped or called explicitly in the relevant test.
